context: Tests whether the agent implements the referral fraud detection function with the correct set of signals, correct blocking decisions for each signal, proper corporate email domain logic, IP cluster threshold, and timing-based signals.

checklist.name: Same shipping address blocks
checklist.description: A 'same_shipping_address' (or equivalent) signal is returned with block: true when both referrer and referee share the same shipping address

checklist.name: Same address uses hash comparison
checklist.description: The shipping address comparison uses a hash field (not string comparison of raw address fields) to detect duplicates

checklist.name: Corporate email domain signal
checklist.description: A 'same_corporate_email_domain' (or equivalent) signal is returned when referrer and referee share the same non-consumer email domain

checklist.name: Corporate email is non-blocking
checklist.description: The same-email-domain signal has block: false (does NOT block automatically)

checklist.name: Consumer domains excluded
checklist.description: The email domain check excludes at least gmail.com, yahoo.com, hotmail.com, and outlook.com from triggering the corporate domain signal

checklist.name: IP cluster signal
checklist.description: A signal is returned when the referrer has more than 3 referrals from the same IP range within the past 30 days

checklist.name: IP cluster is non-blocking
checklist.description: The IP cluster signal has block: false

checklist.name: Immediate signup signal
checklist.description: A signal is returned when the referee account was created within 5 minutes of the most recent referral click

checklist.name: Immediate signup is non-blocking
checklist.description: The immediate-signup signal has block: false

checklist.name: FraudSignal interface
checklist.description: The return type or interface for each signal includes both a 'signal' string field and a 'block' boolean field

checklist.name: 30-day IP lookback window
checklist.description: The IP cluster check uses a 30-day lookback window (not a different duration)
